summary: Drew DeVault provides his May 2021 status update covering personal news, SourceHut development, and his secret programming language project. He reports completing the first version of the builds.sr.ht GraphQL API and outlines the remaining services needing GraphQL APIs. He shares a code sample from his then-secret language (Hare) demonstrating Linux io_uring bindings, noting the low-level API will be wrapped by a portable event loop interface called 'iobus' in the standard library. He also mentions improvements to godocs.io by contributor Adnan Maolood.
key_points: The builds.sr.ht GraphQL API shipped its first version, with write functionality and other service APIs to follow
A code sample from DeVault's secret language project (Hare) shows io_uring integration with explicit error handling and match expressions
The low-level io_uring API will be wrapped by a portable event loop interface called 'iobus' in the standard library
godocs.io received improvements including a Gemini protocol version
money_quotes: The next one up for a GraphQL API will probably be lists.sr.ht. After that it's just man.sr.ht, paste.sr.ht, and dispatch.sr.ht — all three of which are pretty small.
The low-level io_uring API will be wrapped by a portable event loop interface (tentatively named "iobus") in the standard library.
I'm planning on using this to write a finger server.
key_insight: SourceHut's GraphQL migration is nearing completion while DeVault's secret language project (Hare) is taking shape with low-level Linux io_uring bindings designed to be wrapped in a portable abstraction.
